message:
fix: update install script to use go install for remote installation

The previous script tried to build from source (cmd/bv/main.go) which
fails when running via curl | bash since there's no local source code.

Changes:
- Use 'go install github.com/.../cmd/bv@latest' to fetch and build
- Add Go version check (requires 1.21+ for @latest syntax)
- Detect GOBIN or GOPATH/bin for installation location
- Add helpful message if GOPATH/bin is not in PATH

This follows the standard Go installation pattern used by most Go
tools and works correctly when piped from curl.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,35 +7,50 @@ print_error() { printf "\033[1;31m==>\033[0m %s\n" "$1"; }
 
 install_bv() {
     local bin_name="bv"
-    local install_path="/usr/local/bin/$bin_name"
+    local repo="github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer"
 
     print_info "Checking requirements..."
     if ! command -v go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-        print_error "Go is not installed. Please install Go (golang.org) to build from source."
+        print_error "Go is not installed. Please install Go (golang.org) first."
         exit 1
     fi
 
-    print_info "Building bv..."
-    # Build to a temporary file first
-    local tmp_bin
-    tmp_bin=$(mktemp)
-    
-    if go build -o "$tmp_bin" cmd/bv/main.go; then
-        print_info "Installing to $install_path..."
-        # Check if we need sudo
-        if [ -w "$(dirname "$install_path")" ]; then
-            mv "$tmp_bin" "$install_path"
+    # Check Go version is at least 1.21 for go install with @latest
+    go_version=$(go version | grep -oP 'go\K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
+    major=$(echo "$go_version" | cut -d. -f1)
+    minor=$(echo "$go_version" | cut -d. -f2)
+    if [ "$major" -lt 1 ] || { [ "$major" -eq 1 ] && [ "$minor" -lt 21 ]; }; then
+        print_error "Go 1.21 or later is required. Found: go$go_version"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    print_info "Installing bv via go install..."
+
+    # Use go install to fetch and build the latest version
+    if go install "$repo/cmd/bv@latest"; then
+        # Find where Go installed it
+        local gobin="${GOBIN:-$(go env GOPATH)/bin}"
+        local installed_path="$gobin/$bin_name"
+
+        if [ -f "$installed_path" ]; then
+            print_success "Successfully installed $bin_name to $installed_path"
+
+            # Check if it's in PATH
+            if ! command -v "$bin_name" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+                print_info "Note: $gobin may not be in your PATH."
+                print_info "Add this to your shell profile:"
+                print_info "  export PATH=\"\$PATH:$gobin\""
+            else
+                print_info "Run '$bin_name' in any beads project to view issues."
+            fi
         else
-            sudo mv "$tmp_bin" "$install_path"
+            print_error "Binary not found at expected location: $installed_path"
+            exit 1
         fi
-        chmod +x "$install_path"
-        print_success "Successfully installed $bin_name!"
-        print_info "Run '$bin_name' in any initialized beads project to view issues."
     else
-        rm -f "$tmp_bin"
-        print_error "Build failed."
+        print_error "Installation failed."
         exit 1
     fi
 }
 
-install_bv
\ No newline at end of file
+install_bv
